english: żé-sa mi śes-pas (zhe sa mi shes pas) because at that time people knew little of compliments and politeness Pth.; żé-sa byéd-pa (zhe sa byed pa) to show honour, respect, rnám-gyur mdzés-pai żé-sa ´bul-ba (rnam gyur mdzes pa'i zhe sa 'bul ba) to arrange mimic performances in honour of some persons, (which also at the present time is frequently done in these countries); complimentary word (for żé-sai tsig (zhe sa'i tshig)), rná-bai żé-sa snyan (rna ba'i zhe sa snyan) the complimentary word for rna-ba (rna ba) is snyan (snyan) Zam.

Cs. (W. *żaṅ* (zhang)) 1. breadth, width, żeṅ-ćan (zheng can) broad (road, valley), wide, spacious, *żeṅ k'a-ćém-po* (zheng kha chem po) C. id.; żeṅ-méd (zheng med), żeṅ-prá-mo (zheng phra mo), *żeṅ-ćúṅ-se* (zheng chung se) W. narrow; żéṅ-du (zheng du) in breadth Sambh.; żeṅ-śiṅ (zheng shing) writing-tablet = snum-glegs (snum glegs). -- 2. plain, surface, side, żeṅ-ćé-ba ynyis (zheng che ba gnyis) the two broad sides (of a pillar) Glr.
to fear, to be afraid, synon. to ´jigs-pa ('jigs pa) Thgr. frq.; żéd-nas (zhed nas) full of apprehensions Pth.

(cf. że (zhe)), vb. c. la (la), 1. to desire, to long for, to be attached to, to be partial to, to be taken with, kyéd-la żén-ćiṅ ćags (khyed la zhen cing chags) I love you ardently (ἐρῶ) Glr.; bod-´báṅs ṅá-la żen-ćé-żiṅ dgá-ba-rnams (bod 'bangs nga la zhen che zhing dga' ba rnams) the people of Tibet, that are affectionately attached to me Glr.; sbst.: desire, longing, e.g. to hear more of a thing Mil.; also greediness, covetousness; ráṅ-´dod-żen-pa (rang 'dod zhen pa) self-love, selfishness, egotism Glr.; pyógs-żen (phyogs zhen) Tar. 184, 22, party-spirit, party-agitation; ćágs-med zén-med (chags med zhen med) free from passion or interest Mil.; żén-pa zlog (zhen pa zlog) suppress your passion Mil.; tse ´dí-la zen ldóg-pa (tshe 'di la zhen ldog pa) to be disgusted with this life Thgy.; *żém-pa ma lóg-na dhe'-pa mi yoṅ* (zhem pa ma log na dad pa mi yong) C. before one has renounced every desire, one cannot believe. -- Comp. żén-k'a (zhen kha), żé-k'a (zhe kha) = żén-pa (zhen pa) sbst., Sch. -- żeṅ-k'rís (zhen khris) Mil., żeṅ-ćágs (zhen chags) frq., also vulgo, żen-´dzín (zhen 'dzin) Glr. inclination, desire, passion, attachment, *żen-dzín ćò-će* (zhen 'dzin co ce) W. to love, to be attached. -- żen-dón (zhen don), resp. bżed-dón (bzhed don), object of desire Cs. -- żen-lóg (zhen log) (-pa (-pa), cf. above), disinclination, antipathy, disgust; in an ascetic sense: resignation Mil.; ´jig-rtén żen-lóg-gi gaṅ-zág ('jig rten zhen log gi gang zag) a man tired of this world Mil.; żen-lóg-pa (zhen log pa), or -mk'an (-mkhan) fastidious, squeamish, easily disgusted; *że-mi-lóg-k'en* (zhe mi log mkhan) C. one that is not easily disgusted, not squeamish. -- 2. = ljén-pa (ljen pa) to penetrate, to be fixed, of colours etc., ras dkar-po tson żen-pa ltar (ras dkar po tshon zhen pa ltar) as a colour is fixed in white cloth, is lasting Dzl.
Ld. an inferior kind of silk, of which the handkerchiefs consist, that are presented to foreign visitors etc. as a welcome or mark of respect, cf k'a-btágs (kha btags).
żer ´débs-par byéd-pa (zher 'debs par byed pa) Cs.: to chide, rebuke, which, however, in the only passage, where I met with the word, does not suit the sense very well.

1. dram, a small weight = 1/8 ounce, of skar-ma (skar ma), v. skár-ma (skar ma); ysér-żo-gaṅ (gser zho gang) Pth. a dram of gold; yser żo ysum-brgyá (gser zho gsum brgya) between 1 and 2 pounds of gold; as a coin it is stated to be = 2/3 rupee. -- 2. resp. ysol-żo (gsol zho) thick milk, curds, żo bsnyál-ba (zho bsnyal ba) to place milk to curdle; milk in gen., esp. maï nú-żo (ma'i nu zho) Dzl., má-żo (ma zho) col., mother's milk; żo-´túṅ (zho 'thung) during the time of suckling, żo-spáṅs zas zai dús-na (zho spangs zas za'i dus na) after the child has been weaned Med.; żo dkróg-pa (zho dkrog pa), skróg-pa (skrog pa), bsrúb-pa (bsrub pa) to churn, to butter Lex. -- 3. a small white spot, sen-żo (sen zho) on a finger nail, so-żo (so zho) on a tooth Glr. -- 4. num. figure: 141. Comp. żó-ka (zho ka) prob. = żo (zho), Thgy -- żo-skyá (zho skya) Med.? -- żo-ćágs (zho chags) Med.? żo-prúm (zho phrum) Sch.: 'a vessel for thick milk' (?), perh. p'ru (phru). -- żo-rás (zho ras) Med., Sch.: spoiled milk. -- *żo-rí* (zho ri) W., (like rú-ma (ru ma) C.) sour milk, used to acidify new milk; in a gen. sense: ferment, leaven, *żo-dzi* (zho rtsi) Ts. -- żo-śi (zho shi) Sch. = żo-rás (zho ras). -- żo-srí (zho sri), żoi spris-ma (zho'i spris ma) Wdn. cream.

1. force, efficiency Cs. -- 2. n. of a medicinal fruit, żo-śá ysum (zho sha gsum), viz. mkál-żo-śa (mkhal zho sha) kidney-shaped, healing diseases of the kidneys (in W. the chesnut bears this name), snyíṅ-żo-śa (snying zho sha) heart-shaped, healing diseases of the heart; gla-gor-żo-śa (gla gor zho sha) is said to be given to horses; besides mćin-pa- (mchin pa)
